task_completion.description: Generate an ideal alpha-helical model of the specified sequence using PyMOL's internal builder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL viewer should display a 3D alpha-helical structure. The sequence viewer or command history (e.g., using the 'fab' command) should show the sequence KWKLFKKIGIGKFLHSAKKF being built.

task_completion.description: Color the structure to highlight its amphipathic nature according to the instructions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The helix in the PyMOL viewer must be colored with three distinct colors: yellow or orange (hydrophobic residues), blue (basic residues), and green (polar residues).

task_completion.description: Measure the end-to-end length of the helix between the C-alpha atoms of residue 1 and 20.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashed measurement line should span the entire length of the helix in the PyMOL viewer, connecting the ends. A distance label showing a value between 27.0 and 30.0 (ideally ~28.7) should be visible.

task_completion.description: Orient the helix to display its distinct hydrophobic and hydrophilic faces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D structure in the PyMOL viewer should be rotated so that the blue residues (hydrophilic/basic) are visibly clustered on one side of the helix, and the yellow/orange residues (hydrophobic) are clustered on the opposite side.

task_completion.description: Save a ray-traced image and export the 3D coordinates as a PDB file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for PyMOL command line history (e.g., 'png', 'save' commands), terminal commands, or a file manager showing the creation of 'magainin_amphipathic.png' in /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/ and 'magainin_ideal.pdb' in /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/.

task_completion.description: Write a plain-text report containing the sequence, measured distance, and basic residue count.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (like 'cat') displaying the contents of '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/peptide_report.txt'. It must explicitly contain the sequence 'KWKLFKKIGIGKFLHSAKKF', a distance value between 27.0 and 30.0, and the basic residue count of 8.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used PyMOL's internal builder and did not download external PDB files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the command history or terminal. The agent should use commands like 'fab' or the PyMOL Build menu. There should be no 'fetch' commands in PyMOL, nor 'wget'/'curl' commands in the terminal for PDB files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually interacted with PyMOL to generate the structure and image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PyMOL GUI should be visible in the screenshots with the structure actively being manipulated, colored, and measured, rather than the agent just writing a script and faking the output files.

integrity.description: Verify the distance reported in the text file matches the actual measurement taken in PyMOL.
integrity.visual_evidence: The distance value written in 'peptide_report.txt' should exactly match the distance label shown on the measurement line in the PyMOL viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target sequence is exactly 'KWKLFKKIGIGKFLHSAKKF'. The correct basic residue count (Lys + His) is 8. The expected end-to-end CA-CA distance for an ideal 20-residue alpha helix is approximately 28.7 Angstroms (acceptable range: 27.0 to 30.0 Angstroms). The agent must use PyMOL's internal builder, not download an existing PDB.
